plain_definition: The EU's securities-markets regulator, and the lead EU-level body for most of MiCA's crypto-asset service provider (CASP), trading-platform, and market-abuse provisions, as well as the DLT Pilot Regime. ESMA writes technical standards and guidelines that bind national competent authorities and, in some cases, firms directly; maintains the EU-wide interim register of authorised CASPs and of entities providing crypto-asset services without authorisation; and coordinates NCA supervisory practice, including through Common Supervisory Actions. ESMA does not itself authorise or supervise individual firms -- that remains an NCA function under national administrative procedure. It plays a broadly similar EU-level standard-setting role to how Hong Kong's SFC or the UK's FCA set rules for securities-type activity, but ESMA sits above, rather than in place of, the 27 member-state regulators.
formal_definition: The European Securities and Markets Authority, established under Regulation (EU) No 1095/2010 (the ESMA Regulation), is the EU-level authority for MiCA Title V (CASP authorisation and operating conditions) and Title VI (market abuse) technical standards and guidelines, and for Regulation (EU) 2022/858 (the DLT Pilot Regime)'s Article 14 review function.
